apple_apple_scab.description: Apple scab causes dark olive lesions on leaves and fruits, leading to premature leaf drop and cosmetic fruit damage.
apple_apple_scab.treatment: Use protective fungicides such as captan or mancozeb during susceptible growth stages.
apple_apple_scab.prevention: Sanitize fallen leaves, prune for airflow, and follow a preventive spray schedule in wet seasons.
apple_apple_scab.fertilizer: Maintain balanced nutrition to improve plant immunity. Avoid excessive nitrogen application which can promote succulent growth susceptible to infections.

apple_black_rot.description: Black rot causes leaf spots, cankers, and fruit rot, often starting from infected dead wood.
apple_black_rot.treatment: Prune and destroy infected branches and mummified fruits. Apply labeled fungicides during active infection periods.
apple_black_rot.prevention: Maintain orchard sanitation, avoid tree stress, and remove dead or wounded tissue promptly.
apple_black_rot.fertilizer: Maintain balanced nutrition to improve plant immunity. Avoid excessive nitrogen application which can promote succulent growth susceptible to infections.

apple_cedar_apple_rust.description: Apple Cedar Apple Rust can cause significant damage to the plant foliage and reduce overall yield if left untreated.
apple_cedar_apple_rust.treatment: Apply recommended fungicides or pesticides for Cedar Apple Rust. Remove and destroy heavily infected plant parts immediately.
apple_cedar_apple_rust.prevention: Practice crop rotation, ensure adequate spacing for airflow, and sanitize tools to prevent Cedar Apple Rust.
apple_cedar_apple_rust.fertilizer: Maintain balanced nutrition to improve plant immunity. Avoid excessive nitrogen application which can promote succulent growth susceptible to infections.

cherry_powdery_mildew.description: Powdery mildew appears as white powder-like fungal growth on leaf surfaces, reducing photosynthesis and fruit quality.
cherry_powdery_mildew.treatment: Apply sulfur or potassium bicarbonate fungicide at recommended intervals. Remove heavily infected leaves.
cherry_powdery_mildew.prevention: Improve canopy airflow through pruning, avoid overhead irrigation, and use resistant varieties when possible.
cherry_powdery_mildew.fertilizer: Maintain balanced nutrition to improve plant immunity. Avoid excessive nitrogen application which can promote succulent growth susceptible to infections.

corn_cercospora_leaf_spot.fertilizer: Maintain balanced nutrition to improve plant immunity. Avoid excessive nitrogen application which can promote succulent growth susceptible to infections.

corn_common_rust.description: Common rust forms reddish-brown pustules on corn leaves and can reduce yield under severe pressure.
corn_common_rust.treatment: Apply fungicide if disease pressure is high before tasseling, based on local extension guidance.
corn_common_rust.prevention: Plant rust-tolerant hybrids and avoid late planting in regions with regular rust outbreaks.
corn_common_rust.fertilizer: Maintain balanced nutrition to improve plant immunity. Avoid excessive nitrogen application which can promote succulent growth susceptible to infections.

corn_northern_leaf_blight.description: Northern leaf blight creates long gray-green lesions that expand and reduce leaf function.
corn_northern_leaf_blight.treatment: Use triazole or strobilurin fungicides where economically justified.
corn_northern_leaf_blight.prevention: Rotate crops, manage residue, and select resistant hybrids to reduce inoculum carryover.
corn_northern_leaf_blight.fertilizer: Maintain balanced nutrition to improve plant immunity. Avoid excessive nitrogen application which can promote succulent growth susceptible to infections.

healthy_apple.description: The Healthy plant appears healthy with no visible signs of disease.
healthy_apple.treatment: No treatment required. Continue regular monitoring.

potato_early_blight.description: Early blight produces concentric ring lesions on older leaves and can lead to defoliation.

potato_early_blight.prevention: Rotate crops, use healthy seed tubers, and avoid prolonged leaf wetness.
potato_early_blight.fertilizer: Maintain balanced nutrition to improve plant immunity. Avoid excessive nitrogen application which can promote succulent growth susceptible to infections.

potato_late_blight.description: Late blight is a fast-spreading water mold disease causing dark lesions and rapid crop collapse in humid weather.

sugarcane_red_rot.treatment: Apply recommended fungicides or pesticides for Red Rot. Remove and destroy heavily infected plant parts immediately.
sugarcane_red_rot.prevention: Practice crop rotation, ensure adequate spacing for airflow, and sanitize tools to prevent Red Rot.
sugarcane_red_rot.fertilizer: Maintain balanced nutrition to improve plant immunity. Avoid excessive nitrogen application which can promote succulent growth susceptible to infections.

tomato_early_blight.description: Tomato early blight causes target-like lesions on older foliage, reducing plant vigor and yield.
tomato_early_blight.treatment: Spray recommended fungicides and remove infected lower leaves.
tomato_early_blight.prevention: Mulch soil, rotate crops, and irrigate at the base to keep foliage dry.
tomato_early_blight.fertilizer: Maintain balanced nutrition to improve plant immunity. Avoid excessive nitrogen application which can promote succulent growth susceptible to infections.

tomato_late_blight.description: Tomato late blight causes large water-soaked lesions and white sporulation under humid conditions.
tomato_late_blight.treatment: Use targeted fungicides early and rogue heavily infected plants.
tomato_late_blight.prevention: Ensure spacing for airflow, avoid overhead watering, and remove volunteer host plants.
tomato_late_blight.fertilizer: Maintain balanced nutrition to improve plant immunity. Avoid excessive nitrogen application which can promote succulent growth susceptible to infections.
